sections.paragraph:
m. contr. from כֵּנֶס from the root כָּנַס, like כֹּוס (which see), and כֹּנֶס a purse, bag (Syr. and Arab. id.), in which money was kept, Pro 1:14 Isa 46:6 and in which traders were accustomed to carry about their weights for weighing goods (see Chardin, Voyage, tom. iii. p. 420), Deu 25:13 Mic 6:11. Hence אַבְנֵי כִיס, Pro 16:11.

[“(2) a cup, i.q. כֹּוס Pro 23:31, כ׳.”]
